hero_subtitle: SFTY helps regulators organize suspicious activity alerts into connected cases with evidence-grade logs and audit-ready reporting for streamlined FIU investigation workflows.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Investigation workflows slow across disconnected data
problem_description: Regulators face delays and gaps when suspicious activity signals, case evidence and reporting remain isolated across multiple systems. This fragmentation makes it difficult to trace investigation steps, support evidence integrity and maintain timely supervisory action.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY connects suspicious activity signals, case evidence, reviewer actions and reporting data into one secure workflow designed to support traceable, timely and defensible FIU investigations.
workflow_title: From market signal to supervisory action
workflow_description: SFTY connects market signals through alert generation, case creation, investigation, supervisory decisions and reporting to support structured regulatory workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. A supervisory control plane
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented market signals, alerts, cases, evidence and reports into one structured supervisory layer for better investigation and regulatory oversight.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY delivers a secure, auditable supervisory layer designed for regulators requiring controlled access, evidence-grade logging and procurement-safe, phased deployment environments.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support investigation workflows for regulators
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how alerts connect to case creation and review
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging preserves investigation audit trails
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe multi-provider orchestration in FIU supervisory workflows
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for AML and FIU teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY helps AML and FIU teams connect alerts, cases, risk data and supervisory records into structured workflows for clearer investigation and reporting.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage investigation workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes alerts through classification, case creation, review, evidence collection and escalation to support a smooth investigation lifecycle.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve investigation evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and supporting evidence with evidence-grade logging for audit and supervisory review readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government systems?
faq_4_answer: SFTY supports modular integration with government registries, analytics providers and reporting systems under controlled access and secure deployment.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace current analytics or tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates as a supervisory layer above existing analytics and compliance tools without replacing the underlying providers or software.
final_title: Structure AML investigation workflows with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects suspicious activity alerts into evidence-backed cases and audit-ready supervisory reports.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
